content:
# Joshua Chapter 1 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), folio page 226 | **Source Image:** `../../source-texts/hebrew-OT/JOS_001_page_001.jpg`

1. And it was after the death of Moshe, servant of YHWH, and YHWH spoke to Yehoshua son of Nun, the minister of Moshe, saying,

2. "Moshe my servant is dead. And now, arise, cross over this Yarden, you and all this people, to the land which I [am] giving to them, to the sons of Yisrael.

3. Every place upon which the sole of your foot shall tread, to you I have given it, as I spoke to Moshe.

4. From the wilderness and this Levanon and as far as the great river, the river Perat, all the land of the Chittites, and as far as the great sea [toward] the setting of the sun — it shall be your border.

5. No man shall stand before you all the days of your life. As I was with Moshe, I will be with you; I will not fail you and I will not forsake you.

6. Be strong and be courageous, for you shall cause this people to inherit the land which I swore to their fathers to give to them.

7. Only be strong and be very courageous, to keep to do according to all the Torah which Moshe my servant commanded you. Do not turn from it [to the] right or [to the] left, in order that you may act wisely in every [place] where you go.

8. This scroll of the Torah shall not depart from your mouth, and you shall meditate in it day and night, in order that you may keep to do according to all that is written in it, for then you shall make your way prosperous, and then you shall act wisely.

9. Have I not commanded you? Be strong and be courageous. Do not be terrified and do not be dismayed, for YHWH your God [is] with you in every [place] where you go."

10. And Yehoshua commanded the officers of the people, saying,

11. "Pass through the midst of the camp and command the people, saying, 'Prepare for yourselves provisions, for in yet three days you [are] crossing over this Yarden to go in to possess the land which YHWH your God [is] giving to you to possess it.'"

12. And to the Reuvenites and to the Gadites and to the half-tribe of Menasheh, Yehoshua spoke, saying,

13. "Remember the word which Moshe, servant of YHWH, commanded you, saying, 'YHWH your God [is] giving rest to you, and he will give to you this land.'

14. Your wives, your little ones, and your livestock shall dwell in the land which Moshe gave to you on the other side of the Yarden; and you shall cross over before your brothers, armed, all the mighty [men] of valor, and you shall help them,

15. until YHWH gives rest to your brothers as [he has given] to you, and they also possess the land which YHWH your God [is] giving to them. And [then] you shall return to the land of your possession and you shall possess it, which Moshe, servant of YHWH, gave to you on the other side of the Yarden, [toward the] rising of the sun."

16. And they answered Yehoshua, saying, "All which you have commanded us we will do, and to every [place] which you send us we will go.

17. According to all which we listened to Moshe, so we will listen to you. Only, may YHWH your God be with you as he was with Moshe.

18. Every man who rebels against your mouth and does not listen to your words, to all which you command him — he shall be put to death. Only be strong and be courageous."

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

**Manuscript and Source:** Translated from Leningrad Codex (B19A), folio 226, as photographed by the West Semitic Research Project (Bruce E. Zuckerman). The Hebrew text of Joshua 1 begins in the rightmost column of the three-column layout, following the final masorah of Deuteronomy and the book title ספר יהושע. Secondary reference: Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), folio 164, for the LXX Greek text of Joshua (ΙΗΣΟΥΣ).

**Verse 1 — ויהי:** The opening wayyiqtol construction וַיְהִי אַחֲרֵי מוֹת מֹשֶׁה ("And it was after the death of Moshe") is a standard narrative-continuation formula linking Joshua to Deuteronomy 34. The waw-consecutive is preserved as "And." The designation מְשָׁרֵת מֹשֶׁה ("minister of Moshe") for Yehoshua uses the Piel participle of שׁרת, indicating ongoing personal service — not a military or priestly office. LXX renders ὑπουργός (assistant/attendant), confirming this nuance.

**Verse 4 — פְּרָת (Perat):** The Leningrad Codex reads נְהַר פְּרָת, the river Perat (Euphrates). Transliterated rather than translated, as is consistent with EMET practice for proper nouns. The LXX reads τοῦ ποταμοῦ τοῦ μεγάλου Εὐφράτου.

**Verse 4 — חִתִּים (Chittites):** The Masoretic text includes כֹּל אֶרֶץ הַחִתִּים ("all the land of the Chittites"), which is absent from the LXX Vaticanus text. This is a significant textual variant. The LXX omits any reference to the Hittites in this verse, suggesting possible later expansion in the MT tradition, or alternatively, haplography in the Greek Vorlage.

**Verse 5 — אֶרְפְּךָ...אֶעֶזְבֶךָּ:** Two verbs of abandonment: לֹא אַרְפְּךָ ("I will not fail you" / "I will not let you go slack") from רפה, and וְלֹא אֶעֶזְבֶךָּ ("and I will not forsake you") from עזב. The doubling intensifies the promise. LXX renders οὐκ ἐγκαταλείψω σε οὐδὲ ὑπερόψομαί σε.

**Verse 7 — תַּשְׂכִּיל:** The Hiphil of שׂכל, rendered "act wisely," carries the sense of prudent success — wisdom demonstrated in outcomes, not merely possessed. The same root appears in verse 8 (תַּצְלִיחַ for "make prosperous" from צלח, and תַּשְׂכִּיל again for "act wisely").

**Verse 8 — וְהָגִיתָ בוֹ:** "And you shall meditate in it" — the verb הגה denotes murmuring, low recitation aloud, not silent contemplation. The Torah was to be spoken, not merely read internally.

**Verse 11 — צֵידָה:** "Provisions" — literally food prepared for a journey. The three-day timeframe (בְּעוֹד שְׁלֹשֶׁת יָמִים) echoes the reconnaissance period of Joshua 2.

**Verse 14 — חֲמֻשִׁים:** Rendered "armed." The precise meaning is debated: it may derive from חמשׁ ("five"), possibly referring to battle formation in five divisions, or more likely from a root meaning "equipped/armed for battle." LXX reads εὔζωνοι ("well-girded/lightly armed"), supporting the martial sense.

**Verse 15 — מִזְרַח הַשָּׁמֶשׁ:** Literally "rising of the sun," the standard Hebrew expression for "east." Preserved literally rather than replacing with "east."

**Verse 18 — יוּמָת:** Hophal imperfect of מות — "he shall be put to death." This is a judicial formula: the passive construction indicates communal execution, not private vengeance. The Transjordanian tribes pledge absolute obedience to Yehoshua's authority, mirroring the obedience given to Moshe.

**LXX Vaticanus Notable Variants:** The Greek text at Joshua 1 in Codex Vaticanus (examined on folio 164) is broadly consonant with the MT, with the notable exception of the Chittite reference in verse 4 as discussed above. The LXX also adds at the end of verse 18 the phrase καθότι ἤκουσαν Μωυσῆ ("just as they listened to Moses"), which is not present in the MT but may reflect an alternate Hebrew Vorlage.

— EMET
